message:
chore(beads): update ft-4o5mb issue record (1 line modified, net zero line count change)

Routine beads metadata sync for ft-4o5mb ("[conformance] wa.search /
wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden
roundtrip"). No body changes, no new issues created, no issues closed
— the single modified line is a status/timestamp field update driven
by the surrounding beads-aware workflow. Line count unchanged.

files.patch:
@@ -46,7 +46,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-48ah6","title":"[security] robot CLI: read-only commands bypass policy gates","description":"crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:16819,16907,17775,17843
repro: configure safety so ReadOutput requires approval or is denied, then run `ft robot state --include-text` or `ft robot wait-for <pane> <pattern>`. Both handlers read pane output without calling `authorize_read_or_search_policy`, so they can return text/wait results instead of `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`. `ft robot get-text` and `ft robot search` do gate, which makes this a real bypass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:03:46.723712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:35:48.162827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:35:48.161759Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 1e82de9a: added authorize_read_or_search_policy gates to RobotCommands::WaitFor (single-pane ReadOutput) and RobotCommands::State --include-text (fleet-level ReadOutput, pane_id=None). Both gates mirror the established GetText pattern at line 17103-17177 — same audit via record_read_search_policy_audit, same error mapping via robot_policy_error_from_decision returning robot.policy_denied / robot.require_approval. Pre-existing 12 compile errors in main.rs (private select! macro + missing StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers/DiagnosticOptions.include_full_cwd struct fields) block end-to-end test verification this pass; all 12 are in unrelated line ranges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-48xy0","title":"storage.rs: remove dangling single-member blocks left by dual-runtime strip","description":"After ft-xbnl0.2.5 (9e1bd63b) stripped all #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] guards, the wrappers survived as empty curly-brace blocks around single return statements. Examples:

crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10543-10548
    {
        let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);
        return self.create_reservation_with_cx(&cx, ...).await;
    }
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10588-10591 (release_reservation)
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs: ~60+ similar occurrences across all cx-routed entry points.

Each site has the form:
    pub async fn foo(...) -> Result<T> {
        {
            let cx = ...;
            return self.foo_with_cx(...).await;
        }
    }

Not a bug. Cosmetic cleanup. Makes storage.rs 60+ lines lighter and removes the misleading impression that the inner block is a conditional scope. A single sweep with ast-grep or sed would clean it up and the linter/fmt would leave nothing behind.

Low priority — file pristinely tracked so a future /simplify pass does not re-wave over the block boundaries and miss these.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:46.113101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.258101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.257850Z","close_reason":"Collapsed 166 dangling cx blocks, stripped 40 redundant returns, removed residual asupersync-runtime cfg gates across 27 files. -504 net lines. cargo check clean. Commit fdd1e817.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.479058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.478891Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:29:03.338350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:29:03.337992Z","close_reason":"Partial verify in 7319bd75: core MCP conformance file landed; rch cargo test remained in compile tail","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"},{"id":3632,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"RoseCat","text":"RoseCat taking over the in-progress conformance handoff. Agent Mail inbox fetch timed out under DB contention after retry, so using Beads for the coordination trail. Scope: inspect the existing MCP conformance helpers and wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for tool flows, identify whether the prior invalid-args blocker leaves a success-envelope coverage path, then land a bounded core-only test/fix if a real gap is reachable.","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:22:55Z"}]}

Verification: cargo fmt --check; git diff --check; ubs --only=rust crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (existing test-harness panic/expect inventory only; embedded cargo check/test-build clean); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rosecut-coretools-target2 rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mcp_conformance_core_tools --features mcp,asupersync-runtime (exit 0).","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:34:15Z"}]}

Cover success and invalid-args envelopes without editing mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:11.123271Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:11.122910Z","close_reason":"Partial verification only; shipped in 4e270e95 after long rch lane. Harness committed; verification incomplete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3619,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocked while landing wa.state conformance harness. Narrow lane used: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-MistyCoast-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test wa_state_mcp_conformance --features mcp,asupersync-runtime. That fell back locally on RCH-E324, then local wrapper reproduced a compile-stop before the new test ran: crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs has non-exhaustive matches for Err(events::RecvError::Cancelled) at lines 574, 586, 749, and 786 (E0004). I stayed inside the assigned slice; only new file is crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs and it remains uncommitted pending baseline compile repair.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:50:51Z"}]}
